novelty_flags.label: Theorem 1
novelty_flags.one_line: Theorem 1 is explicitly a routine population translation of published ICP, anchor-regression, and Causal-Dantzig definitions, not a novel theorem.

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 1
novelty_flags.one_line: RothenhauslerBuhlmannMeinshausen2019CausalDantzig defines inner-product invariance as equality of environment-specific E[X(Y-X'b)] moments; the proposal silently replaces this with Cov(A,Y-X'b)=0 without a Z-encoding or weighting bridge.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=field below novelty_target=flagship: the claimed three-way frontier is not tied to a location-level open problem or strict frontier in the comparator papers beyond the separate equation references.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The Causal-Dantzig block C_A b=d_A is well-defined as an anchor moment, but the proposal does not state the assumptions under which it is exactly the published Causal-Dantzig environment moment equation.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The iff frontier mostly unfolds the deliberately stacked pencil M_S b=h_S plus standard image/kernel facts; the proof route has fewer than three non-routine steps after definitions and assumptions.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add a precise bridge lemma from published Causal-Dantzig environment moments to the anchor covariance equation, including the required encoding of A and any centering or weighting restrictions.

Replace the affine-image iff with an independently characterized rank frontier, such as closed-form determinant/minor conditions that are not obtained merely by stacking the three published equations.
Turn Conjecture 2 into a full minimality theorem for q=p=2 strata, with explicit semi-algebraic inequalities and a proof that q=1 or p=1 cannot separate the invariance notions.

recommended_next_step: Revise the kernel so the flagship claim is an independent rank/minor classification and repair the Causal-Dantzig-to-anchor-moment bridge; otherwise the proposal remains field-tier below the flagship floor.
